prerequisites: A Thinkific site with administrator (Site Owner) access, since API keys are issued from the perspective of the Site Owner.
Your Thinkific site subdomain, which is the first label of your Thinkific URL (for example, yoursite in yoursite.thinkific.com). This populates the required subdomain config field.
A Thinkific API key generated from your site settings. This populates the required, secret api_key config field.

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a Thinkific API key supplied in the api_key secret field, scoped to the site identified by the subdomain field. Thinkific's public REST API (base URL https://api.thinkific.com/api/public/v1) supports API Key authorization, where requests are authorized on behalf of the Thinkific Site Owner. The api_key plus the site subdomain together identify and authorize calls against your site's data.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Thinkific subdomain
setupSteps.body: Determine the subdomain of your Thinkific site. It is the portion of your site URL before .thinkific.com (for example, the subdomain for yoursite.thinkific.com is yoursite). You will enter this value in the connector's required subdomain field so requests target the correct site.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Thinkific API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Thinkific site as an administrator (Site Owner) and generate an API key from your site's settings. Thinkific's public API uses API Key authorization granted on behalf of the Site Owner, so the key must come from an account with administrator access. Copy the generated key.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key in the required, secret api_key field and your site subdomain in the required subdomain field. With both values set, the connector authorizes against the Thinkific public REST API at https://api.thinkific.com/api/public/v1 to read your site's data.

sources.title: Thinkific API documentation - authorization and base URL
